column1: comment_text
The new Companies Act amendments are a welcome step towards better corporate governance
Penalties for non-compliance are too harsh and disproportionate for small companies
The audit requirements need to be simplified for startups and MSMEs
Excellent initiative by MCA to digitize the filing process
The compliance burden on small firms is unreasonable and needs immediate review
Annual return filing process has become much smoother with the new portal
The penalty structure should differentiate between willful default and genuine oversight
Reporting requirements are excessive and create unnecessary paperwork
The new regulations promote transparency and accountability in corporate affairs
Audit standards should be aligned with international best practices
The regulatory framework needs to be more business-friendly
Good step towards improving corporate compliance culture in India
The filing deadlines are too tight for companies with complex structures
MCA should provide more guidance on interpretation of new rules
The digital transformation of MCA services is commendable
Penalties should be reduced for first-time offenders
The new disclosure requirements will help investors make informed decisions
Compliance costs are eating into the profits of small businesses
The e-consultation process itself is a positive step towards inclusive policymaking
More clarity needed on the applicability of new audit provisions
